Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Stan Flowers Company, Inc.
Off Calendar Nov-27-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Stan Flowers Company, Inc.
Dept. 514. Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Janine M. Fiel-Cosse, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Junell Gill, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of George Lucas, Deceased.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Stan Flowers Company, Inc.
Plaintiff's complaint was filed on December 30, 2016.
Amendment to plaintiff's complaint to substitute Stan Flowers Company, Inc. for DOE 301 was filed on January 17, 2017.
Proof of Service of Summons, Complaint and Statement of Damages was filed with the Clerk's Office on February 24, 2017.
Statement of Damages as to defendant Stan Flowers Company, Inc. was filed on March 21, 2017. Default as to defendant Stan Flowers Company, Inc. was entered on March 21, 2017.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on November 19, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Junell Gill, as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of George Lucas, Deceased, and against the Defaulted Defendant Stan Flowers Company, Inc., as follows:

Economic Damages - $760,000.00.
Minus 27% attributable to Triple A Machine Shop, Inc. - ($205,200.00).
Total - $554,800.00.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The W.W. Henry Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-12-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Molly A. Friend of WILSON ELSER MOSKOWITZ EDELMAN & DICKER LLP (415-433-0990), appearing for defendant / moving party The W.W. Henry Company
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party George Lucas

RULING:
DEFENDANT THE W.W. HENRY COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant The W.W. Henry Company, L.P.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. As to the issue of whether the Henry roofing product at issue contained asbestos, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's Separate Statement did not demonstrate that plaintiff failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his contention of liability. Additionally, the deposition testimony excerpts fail to demonstrate that plaintiff does not know the number designation for the Henry's product at issue. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence to negate an essential element of plaintiff's claims. The declaration of Lori Angelo is insufficient to demonstrate that the Henry's product at issue was more likely than not a non-asbestos-containing product. As to the causation issue, defendant sustained its initial burden. Dr. Richard Cohen's declaration creates a triable issue whether exposure to defendant's product was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff's risk of developing cancer. An expert declaration in opposition to a summary judgment motion must be liberally construed. (Garrett v. Howmedica Osteonics Corp. (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 173.) Plaintiff's objections numbers 1, 2, 3 and 6 are sustained and numbers 4, 5 and 7 are overruled. Defendant's objections to Dr. Cohen's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. RULING:
DEFENDANT TRIPLE A MACHINE SHOP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Triple A Machine Shop, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the Negligence II cause of action and off calendar as to the Negligence I and Products Liability causes of action. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The excerpts of witness Frank Taforo's deposition testimony submitted by defendant are not sufficient to demonstrate that Mr. Taforo does not have knowledge supporting plaintiff's claims. This precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant also failed to sustain its initial burden because it did not demonstrate that plaintiff's Negligence II cause of action must fail because plaintiff did not work at a Triple A premise. Plaintiff has dismissed his first (Negligence I) and second (Products Liability) causes of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported (503/CML)
